rationale: Karim's personal assistant demands a bespoke, high-end 'equipment' aesthetic. We reject sci-fi tropes (blue holograms, glassmorphism) in favor of stark, unyielding clarity. Dark mode minimizes eye strain; the UI uses deep obsidian with signal red accents to feel like a high-performance command center, not a chatbot.

typography.rationale: Cabinet Grotesk provides a confident, sharp, bespoke voice for UI labels, while JetBrains Mono handles conversational output, system logs, and action payloads to ground the interface in technical reality.

layout.spacing_scale: Generous internally, tight structurally. Containers use p-6 to p-8, but grid gaps are gap-4 to create a dense command center feel.
patterns: Left Sidebar for capability grid and hardware-like toggles.
Center-Right for the live conversation and action payload cards.

rules: No rounded corners. Use rounded-none for a mechanical feel.
No soft box-shadows. Use 1px solid borders (border-[#222222]).
Hover states should invert colors or use a 1px inner border of Signal Red.
Never use glassmorphism. It feels flimsy. Use solid opaque backgrounds.

voice_input.style: Mechanical toggle. Stark white background when idle, stark Signal Red background when active/listening. Sharp corners. text-black when active.
voice_input.animation: Snapping, immediate transition. No soft fading.

action_card.style: Looks like a terminal output block. bg-[#0A0A0A], 1px border. Uses monospace font exclusively. Keys are grey, values are white.

waveform_indicator.style: Minimalist vertical bars, pure white or signal red. NO generic gradients. Syncs with TTS/Mic.
waveform_indicator.animation: Staggered height scaling based on audio frequency (simulated).

chat_message.jarvis: Align left, white monospace text, accompanied by a small status dot (red for action, white for speech).

rules: No easing delays. Use 'ease-out' for everything.
Action cards snap into place with staggered opacity.
Typewriter effect for JARVIS spoken responses (critical for the 'assistant' feel).

waveform_texture.description: Abstract metallic waveform. Use as a very subtle background for the capability sidebar or idle state overlay.

obsidian_status_bg.description: High-end matte metal with red LED slit. Use as the background for the primary system status header.

icons.usage: Use sharp, un-rounded icons if possible, or stick to bare minimum technical symbols (Terminal, Mic, Activity, Power). Keep stroke width at 1.5 or 1.

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Exceeds APCA requirements. White on #050505 is highly legible.
accessibility_and_testing.testing_attributes: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid. Examples: data-testid='mic-toggle-button', data-testid='action-card-sms', data-testid='system-status-indicator'.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a single-page application that feels like a full-screen hardware terminal.
Do NOT use generic rounded chat bubbles. The conversation stream should look like an append-only log.
When an Action is parsed (e.g., Send SMS), render it as a distinct visual 'Card' (Action Payload block) next to or inside the conversation log, displaying the JSON parameters.
Implement a live visualizer (waveform or bar visualizer) that reacts to the system 'listening' or 'speaking' states.
Keep standard Shadcn components to a minimum; override their borders to be rounded-none and strip shadows.
Include an overarching layout grid: Left sidebar (1/4 width) for persistent system status (Online, Network, Modules) and right main area (3/4 width) for the interaction log.
Ensure the application state manages: IDLE, LISTENING, PROCESSING, SPEAKING.
